sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
player = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']

print(f"Green: {len(green)}")
for g in green:
    b = g['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in b)
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in b)
    print(f"  ({min_r},{min_c})")

if player:
    pb = player[0]['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in pb)
    min_c = min(pt[1] for pt in pb)
    print(f"Player: ({min_r},{min_c})")

# Check if we're in initial or new state
# Initial state has green at rows 25 and 31
# New state has green at rows 1, 7, 37, 43
rows = set()
for g in green:
    b = g['boundary']
    min_r = min(pt[0] for pt in b)
    rows.add(min_r)
print(f"Green rows: {sorted(rows)}")

if 25 in rows or 31 in rows:
    print("State: INITIAL")
else:
    print("State: NEW")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, clear the initial remaining block at (31, 13)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 13}])

# Now try: middle 4 (left to right), move LEFT, bottom 4 (right to left)
middle = [(37, 13), (37, 19), (37, 25), (37, 31)]
bottom = [(43, 31), (43, 25), (43, 19), (43, 13)]

actions = []
for r, c in middle:
    actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c})
actions.append('LEFT')
for r, c in bottom:
    actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c})

action(actions)

# Check state

nodes = seg['nodes']
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
result = last_action_result
print(f"Green: {len(green)}, game_over={result.get('game_over', False)}, level_completed={result.get('level_completed', False)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
